an10.94:1.1: I have heard that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Campā on the shore of Gaggarā Lake.
an10.94:1.2: Then Vajjiya Māhita the householder left Campā in the middle of the day to see the Blessed One,
an10.94:1.3: but then the thought occurred to him,
an10.94:1.4: “Now is not the right time to see the Blessed One,
an10.94:1.6: And it is not the right time to see the mind-developing monks,
an10.94:1.7: for they too are in seclusion.
an10.94:1.8: Why don’t I visit the park of the wanderers of other persuasions?”
an10.94:2.1: So he headed to the park of the wanderers of other persuasions.
an10.94:2.2: Now on that occasion the wanderers of other persuasions had come together in a gathering and were sitting, discussing many kinds of bestial topics, making a great noise & racket.
an10.94:3.1: They saw Vajjiya Māhita the householder coming from afar, and on seeing him, hushed one another:
an10.94:3.2: “Be quiet, good sirs. Don’t make any noise.
an10.94:3.3: Here comes Vajjiya Māhita the householder, a disciple of the contemplative Gotama.
an10.94:3.4: He is one of those disciples of the contemplative Gotama, clad in white, who lives in Campā.
an10.94:3.5: These people are fond of quietude, trained in quietude, and speak in praise of quietude.
an10.94:3.6: Maybe, if he perceives our group as quiet, he will consider it worth his while to come our way.”
an10.94:4.2: Then Vajjiya Māhita the householder went to where the wanderers of other persuasions were staying. On arrival he greeted them courteously.
an10.94:4.3: After an exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, he sat to one side. As he was sitting there, the wanderers said to him,
an10.94:4.4: “Is it true, householder, that the contemplative Gotama criticizes all asceticism, that he categorically denounces & disparages all ascetics who live the rough life?”
an10.94:4.5: “No, venerable sirs, the Blessed One does not criticize all asceticism, nor does he categorically denounce or disparage all ascetics who live the rough life.
an10.94:4.6: The Blessed One criticizes what should be criticized, and praises what should be praised.
an10.94:4.7: Criticizing what should be criticized, praising what should be praised, the Blessed One is one who speaks making distinctions,
an10.94:4.8: not one who speaks categorically on this matter.”
an10.94:5.1: When this was said, one of the wanderers said to Vajjiya Māhita the householder,
an10.94:5.2: “Now wait a minute, householder. This contemplative Gotama whom you praise is a nihilist, one who doesn’t declare anything.”
an10.94:5.3: “I tell you, venerable sirs, that the Blessed One righteously declares that
an10.94:5.6: Declaring that ‘This is skillful’ and ‘This is unskillful,’ he is one who has declared (a teaching).
an10.94:5.7: He is not a nihilist, one who doesn’t declare anything.”
an10.94:6.1: When this was said, the wanderers fell silent, abashed, sitting with their shoulders drooping, their heads down, brooding, at a loss for words.
an10.94:6.2: Vajjiya Māhita the householder, perceiving that the wanderers were silent, abashed…at a loss for words, got up & went to the Blessed One. On arrival, having bowed down to the Blessed One, he sat to one side.
an10.94:6.3: As he was sitting there, he told the Blessed One the entirety of his conversation with the wanderers.
an10.94:7.1: (The Blessed One said:) “Well done, householder. Well done.
an10.94:7.2: That is how you should periodically & righteously refute those foolish men.
an10.94:7.3: I don’t say that all asceticism is to be pursued,
an10.94:7.4: nor do I say that all asceticism is not to be pursued.
an10.94:7.5: I don’t say that all observances should be observed,
an10.94:7.6: nor do I say that all observances should not be observed.
an10.94:7.7: I don’t say that all exertions are to be pursued,
an10.94:7.8: nor do I say that all exertions are not to be pursued.
an10.94:7.9: I don’t say that all forfeiture should be forfeited,
an10.94:7.10: nor do I say that all forfeiture should not be forfeited.
an10.94:7.11: I don’t say that all release is to be used for release,
an10.94:7.12: nor do I say that all release is not to be used for release.
an10.94:8.1: “If, when an asceticism is pursued, unskillful qualities grow and skillful qualities wane, then I tell you that that sort of asceticism is not to be pursued.
an10.94:8.2: But if, when an asceticism is pursued, unskillful qualities wane and skillful qualities grow, then I tell you that that sort of asceticism is to be pursued.
an10.94:9.1: “If, when an observance is observed, unskillful qualities grow and skillful qualities wane, then I tell you that that sort of observance is not to be observed.
an10.94:9.2: But if, when an observance is observed, unskillful qualities wane and skillful qualities grow, then I tell you that that sort of observance is to be observed.
an10.94:12.1: “If, when a release is used for release, unskillful qualities grow and skillful qualities wane, then I tell you that that sort of release is not to be used for release.
an10.94:12.2: But if, when a release is used for release, unskillful qualities wane and skillful qualities grow, then I tell you that that sort of release is to be used for release.”
an10.94:13.1: When Vajjiya Māhita the householder had been instructed, urged, roused & encouraged by the Blessed One with a talk on Dhamma, he got up from his seat and, having bowed down to the Blessed One, left, keeping the Blessed One on his right side.
an10.94:14.1: Not long afterward, the Blessed One addressed the monks:
an10.94:14.2: “Monks, even a monk who has long penetrated the Dhamma in this Dhamma & Vinaya would do well, periodically & righteously, to refute the wanderers of other persuasions in just the way Vajjiya Māhita the householder has done.”
